name: Joe Temperley
abstract: Joe Temperley (20 September 1929 – 11 May 2016) was a Scottish jazz saxophonist. He performed with various instruments, but was most associated with the baritone saxophone, soprano saxophone, and bass clarinet.

interviews.title: Joe Temperley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

concerts.label: Clark Terry and His Big Band

works.t: Blues All Day, Blues All Night

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Mel Torme and George Shearing

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part II
about_quotes.evidence: Joe and I work together a lot... we worked together quite a bit in between times... I can work there with the quartet, you know the trio plus Joe, Joe Temperley.
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part II interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 18, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Alvin Queen
about_quotes.evidence: And we did it in New York with Joe Temperley he played baritone saxophone, he was in there, and Steve Bernstein was in there, and I was playing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alvin Queen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 23, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Vacher
about_quotes.evidence: Whether they have something that is so special that others this side of the pond might learn from it well I’ll leave others to say. But we have produced some fine players if you think of someone like Joe Temperley, presently with the Lincoln Center Jazz Orchestra.
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Vacher interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: I have to tell you I forgot about that really. But that is a very good album specifically because of the giants that are all on there... They play so beautifully
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 9, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Lesberg
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Temperley just talked about Ernie Caceres, he says that oh, he missed him, that he was just so sad about what he used to do.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Lesberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Galloway
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Okay. You probably know Joe Temperley. [INTERVIEWEE] Yes indeed. Oh yes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Galloway interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998
